title: Beacon Biosignals is mapping the brain during sleep
description: Beacon Biosignals is creating a model to help diagnose and treat brain disorders, based on data collected while people sleep at home. The firm was founded by MIT alumnus Jake Donoghue and former MIT researcher Jarrett Revels.

title: Improving understanding with language
description: MIT senior Olivia Honeycutt studies the brain and linguistics to explore how cognition, education, language, language learning, education, policy, and the ways we communicate can shape our views of the world.

title: Making the case for curiosity-driven science
description: President Sally Kornbluth spoke in front of a packed crowd about growing challenges to the U.S. research ecosystem as funding for America’s top research universities becomes increasingly strained

title: Solving the “Whac-a-mole dilemma”: A smarter way to debias AI vision models
description: A new debiasing approach called WRING resolves the "Whac-a-Mole dilemma" of existing debiasing approaches that can create or amplify existing biases.

title: The MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab launches to shape the future of AI and quantum computing
description: IBM and MIT announced the launch of the MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab, advancing their long-standing collaboration to shape the next era of computing that combines AI, algorithms, and quantum computing. The new lab evolved from the MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab.

title: Enabling privacy-preserving AI training on everyday devices | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT researchers developed a technique that accelerates a privacy-preserving approach for training AI models on edge devices. Their new framework could enable more accurate, efficient, and secure AI models to be used in under-resourced settings.

title: A faster way to estimate AI power consumption | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: The EnergAIzer technique can predict how much power a certain AI workload will consume when run on a particular processor. This method could help data center operators and algorithm developers improve the sustainability of AI workloads.

title: MIT scientists build the world’s largest collection of Olympiad-level math problems, and open it to everyone | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT CSAIL scientists have compiled the largest high-quality dataset of proof-based math problems ever created. It can help researchers test AI models’ mathematical reasoning, while capturing the full range of mathematical perspectives and problem-solving traditions within the global math community.
